title: narcissus aureus subsp. bertolonii
intro: Narcissus aureus subsp. bertolonii, also known as the Bertoloni's daffodil, is a plant species in the Amaryllidaceae family, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and slender stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its potential to treat various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to the presence of alkaloids like lycorine and galantamine. The health benefits of Narcissus aureus subsp. bertolonii include its ability to act as an expectorant, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic, making it useful in treating conditions such as bronchitis, coughs, and wounds. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties for therapeutic purposes.
